question: Will Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange exceed 9000 USD/tonne on May 31, 2026?
textDescription:
This market tracks Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange.

Measurement date: May 31, 2026

Resolution:

YES if: Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange ≥ 9000 USD/tonne on 2026-05-31

NO if: Nickel price on the London Metal Exchange < 9000 USD/tonne on 2026-05-31

LME data feed, Metal Bulletin reports

The Indonesian mining quota cut impacts global supplies, with price increases in stainless steel reflecting tightening market conditions.

Rationale: The significant cut at Indonesia’s biggest nickel operation may push up prices due to reduced supply, prompting stainless producers to raise their prices.

Source: Article
newsShortReason: Indonesia's significant nickel mining quota cuts have led to substantial price increases.
newsKeyEvidence: Indonesia reduced its 2026 nickel mining quota by approximately 34% to 205 million wet metric tonnes, down from 379 million in 2025. | This reduction has led to immediate increases in global nickel prices. | The London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel prices have risen sharply following the announcement. | Analysts expect the global nickel surplus to narrow significantly in 2026 due to these cuts. | The International Nickel Study Group (INSG) forecasts a 32,000-tonne deficit for 2026, the first since 2021. | The Indonesian government aims to align mining output with domestic processing capacity and smelter needs. | The reduced supply is expected to support higher nickel prices in the global market. | The LME nickel price reached a two-year high in April 2026, influenced by these supply constraints.
